title: What Kind of Creator Are You?
description: Discover which content format best suits your strengths.

questions.question: When you want to share an idea, you prefer to:
options: Write it down carefully and edit it
Talk about it on camera with visuals
questions.explanation: If you prefer writing, a blog might be your ideal format. Writing allows careful editing and precision.

questions.question: Which opening hook is MOST effective for a blog post about climate change?
options: "Did you know that 2023 was the hottest year in recorded history?"
questions.explanation: A surprising statistic grabs attention immediately and establishes credibility.

questions.question: A strong Call to Action should tell your audience exactly what to ___ next.
questions.explanation: A CTA must be clear and actionable: "Subscribe", "Share this post", "Leave a comment", etc.

questions.question: What is a "niche" in content creation?
options: Your specific topic or area of expertise
questions.explanation: Your niche is the specific area you focus on. Example: "sustainable fashion for young professionals."

questions.question: "What if everything you knew about productivity was wrong?" This hook uses:
questions.explanation: Rhetorical questions make the audience curious and engage their thinking immediately.

questions.question: "___ refers to the likes, comments, and shares your audience gives your content."
questions.explanation: Engagement = audience interaction. High engagement means your content resonates.

questions.question: What should come AFTER your hook in a blog post?
options: Your thesis statement or main point
questions.explanation: After hooking attention, immediately present your main point or thesis to set expectations.

questions.question: Which is the BEST Call to Action for the end of a podcast episode?
options: "If you enjoyed this episode, subscribe and leave a review — it really helps!"
questions.explanation: Specific, friendly, and explains WHY they should act. "It really helps" adds motivation.

questions.question: The first step in content creation is:
options: Brainstorming ideas and choosing your topic
questions.explanation: Always start with brainstorming. A clear topic and plan prevent wasted time and weak content.

questions.question: Your ___ audience is the specific group of people you create content for.
questions.explanation: Target audience = the specific group you aim to reach. Knowing them shapes your tone, topics, and style.

title: The Perfect Hook
description: Build catchy hooks using different techniques.

sentences.prompt: Build a hook using a rhetorical question about productivity
sentences.correct: What if everything you knew about productivity was wrong?

sentences.prompt: Build a hook using a surprising statistic
sentences.correct: According to recent studies, we spend 7 hours daily on screens.

sentences.prompt: Build a hook using a hypothetical scenario
sentences.correct: Imagine a world where you could learn any language in 30 days.

sentences.prompt: Build a strong Call to Action for a podcast
sentences.correct: If you enjoyed this episode, please subscribe and leave a review!

sentences.prompt: Build a hook using a personal anecdote
sentences.correct: Last Tuesday, something happened that completely changed my perspective.

sentences.prompt: Build a CTA for a blog post
sentences.correct: Share this article with someone who needs to hear this!

title: Content Creator Vocabulary
description: Master the professional terminology of the content creation world.

cards.back: The interaction that your audience has with your content: likes, comments, shares. High engagement = content that connects.
cards.example: Our latest post had incredible engagement — over 500 comments!

cards.back: Clear instruction for the audience to do something: subscribe, comment, share, visit a link.
cards.example: If you enjoyed this episode, please subscribe and leave a review!

cards.back: Your topic or area of ​​expertise as a creator. A specific niche attracts a loyal audience.
cards.example: My niche is sustainable fashion for young professionals.

cards.back: The specific group of people you target your content to. Defining it improves your communication.
cards.example: My target audience is English learners aged 20-35.

cards.back: Thumbnail image that represents your video/content. It's the first thing people see.
cards.example: A good thumbnail can increase your click-through rate by 30%.

cards.back: Search Engine Optimization — optimize your content to appear in Google/YouTube searches.
cards.example: Good SEO helps your blog appear on the first page of Google results.

cards.back: Publication plan organized by dates. It keeps you consistent.
cards.example: I use a content calendar to plan my posts two weeks in advance.

cards.back: The first phrase or image that catches your attention. Without a good hook, no one stays.
cards.example: The hook of my video was: 'What if I told you that you're learning English wrong?'

cards.back: Generate income with your content: ads, sponsors, memberships, products.
cards.example: She started monetizing her channel after reaching 10K subscribers.

cards.back: Data on the performance of your content: views, watch time, demographics.
cards.example: According to my analytics, most of my audience watches from mobile devices.

cards.back: Person who follows your channel/account to receive your new content.
cards.example: We just reached 10,000 subscribers! Thank you all!

cards.back: Content that is shared massively in a short time.
cards.example: My video about language learning went viral — 2 million views in one week!

title: Hook Technique → Example
description: Match each type of hook with its specific example.

pairs.left: "Last Tuesday, I was walking home when..."

description: Decipher terms to create your own content.
